critical.mandate: Tests must fail initially — this is the red phase of TDD
Each test maps to exactly one acceptance criterion
Only high-risk stories require ATDD — if no high-risk stories exist, exit gracefully

step.title: Load and Filter High-Risk Stories
step.action: Filter stories tagged as Risk: HIGH
For each high-risk story: extract acceptance criteria (AC1, AC2, ...)
step.check: Report "No high-risk stories found — ATDD not required for this project." and exit workflow.
step.action: Present the list of high-risk stories and their ACs to the user
step.ask: These high-risk stories need ATDD tests. Confirm to proceed with all, or select specific stories. [all / select / skip]

step.title: Convert to Tests
step.action: For each selected high-risk story: transform each AC into a failing acceptance test
One test per AC — maintain clear 1:1 mapping (AC1 → Test 1, AC2 → Test 2, ...)
Name tests descriptively: test name should reflect the AC being validated

step.title: Implement Tests
step.action: Write the test code (red phase — tests must fail because implementation does not exist yet)
Use the project's test framework conventions if detectable
Each test must be atomic, independent, and meaningful — no vacuous assertions

step.title: Validate Structure
step.action: Ensure every AC from the selected stories has exactly one corresponding test
Verify tests are independent — no shared state between tests
Run tests to confirm they all FAIL (red phase validation)

step.title: Generate Output
step.action: For each high-risk story: generate ATDD document with AC-to-test mapping table, test code, and run results showing failures
step.template-output: Generate one ATDD file per high-risk story. Each file includes: story key, story title, risk level, AC-to-test mapping table, test implementations, and test run output confirming all tests fail (red phase).
